sections.paragraph:
pr. i.q. Arab. جدف to cut off (compare under גָּדַד), figuratively to cut with opprobrious words. So-

Piel. גִּדֵּף to reproach, to revile. (Arab. Conj. II. Syr. Pael id.)-(a) men (see גִּדּוּפִים), especially-(b) God, 2Ki 19:6, 22 2Ki 19:22 Isa 37:6, 23 Isa 37:23 Psa 44:17. This may not only be done by words but also in action, when men, by boldly and determinedly sinning, mock God and his law. Num 15:30 Eze 20:27.

Derivatives גְּדוּפָה, גִּדּוּפִים.
